design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed = hash('Personal AI Agent Market Brief|blog|buyers and builders tracking personal AI agents|2026-07-06T02:33:27.932125+00:00') → hero=editorial-split; components=[source-card-dossier, workflow-playbook-timeline, decision-matrix]; GSAP=[pinned-chapter-scroll, hover-physics]. AIDA Check: Attention(hero), Interest(market brief + sources), Desire(workflow media + cache explanation), Action(CTA band). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk. Hero Math Verification: clamp(44px–76px) max-width 1120px → 2–3 lines. Bento Density Verification: compare/grid uses grid-auto-flow:dense. GSAP Motion Selection: guarded ScrollTrigger + hover physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: primary black pill CTA, high-contrast secondary CTA verified.
headline: Personal AI Agent Market Brief

site_brief.audience: buyers and builders tracking personal AI agents
site_brief.goal: Create a frequently refreshed editorial blog homepage with a long-form lead analysis, practical how-to explainer, source cards, and a clear Super perspective on why computer-use caching makes repeated agent work cheaper and better. Make it read like a useful market briefing, not a list of summaries.
site_brief.title: Personal AI Agent Market Brief

summary: A field guide for buyers and builders tracking personal AI agents in 2026—what actually changed this week, how computer-use agents differ across ChatGPT, Gemini, Grok, Siri, Folk, and Orchids, and why Super’s computer-use cache makes repeated workflows cheaper and more reliable.
